issues.title: [web] Password manager icon overlaps the text in TextField
issues.body:
Hi,

Im trying to build a login/sign up form for the Web.

Im using the `autofillHints` option on `TextFormField` to allow password managers to save and fill the textboxes

However, with 1password,, the icon to autofill gets wrongly placed on the textbox: 
instead of being aligned to the right of the textbox it gets in the middle of the textbox
## Steps to Reproduce

Sample code to  reproduce: 
```
MaterialApp(
          home: Scaffold(
            body: Container(
              color: Colors.orangeAccent,
              width: 500,
              child: Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets.all(15.0),
                child: Column(
                  children: [
                    Text('Email'),
                    TextFormField(
                      autofillHints: [AutofillHints.email],
                    ),
                    Text('Password'),
                    TextFormField(
                      autofillHints: [AutofillHints.password],
                    ),
                  ],
                ),
              ),),
           )...
````


**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Expeted the password manager icon to be placed on the right of the text field

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

password manager icon is placed in the middle of the textbox  as shown here:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25303342/116093030-3e961980-a69e-11eb-9217-eb39295eb61a.png) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25303342/116093095-4c4b9f00-a69e-11eb-8797-19d43f8c9398.png)

issues.title: TextEditingController.value.composing is giving wrong results in Web & iOS
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce 1. Create an example app with a TextField. 2. Log its TextEditingController.value.composing. 3. Run in MacOS, type "n", "h", then select "你好" with pinyin IME. 4. Run in Web, type "n", "h", then select "你好" with pinyin IME. ### Expected results After the user selects "你好", The final "composing" TextRange should both be (start: -1, end: -1) in MacOS and Web. ### Actual results In MacOS, the final composing TextRange is logged as (start: -1, end: -1), but in Web, the final composing TextRange is logged as (start: 0, end: 2), ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple), ), home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'), ); } } class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget { const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title}); final String title; @override State createState() => _MyHomePageState(); } class _MyHomePageState extends State { final _controller = TextEditingController(); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( appBar: AppBar( backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.inversePrimary, title: Text(widget.title), ), body: Center( child: Column( mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [ const Text('test'), TextField( controller: _controller, onChanged: (_) { print(_controller.value.composing); }, ), ], ), ), ); } } ```
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration
### Logs
Logs MacOS ```console flutter: TextRange(start: 0, end: 1) flutter: TextRange(start: 0, end: 3) flutter: TextRange(start: -1, end: -1) ``` Web ```console TextRange(start: 0, end: 1) TextRange(start: 0, end: 3) TextRange(start: 0, end: 2) ```
### Flutter Doctor output
Doctor output ```console [✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.35.1, on macOS 14.3.1 23D60 darwin-x64, locale en-GB) [669ms] • Flutter version 3.35.1 on channel stable at /Applications/flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision 20f8274939 (6 days ago), 2025-08-14 10:53:09 -0700 • Engine revision 1e9a811bf8 • Dart version 3.9.0 • DevTools version 2.48.0 • Pub download mirror https://pub.flutter-io.cn • Flutter download mirror https://storage.flutter-io.cn • Feature flags: enable-web, enable-linux-desktop, enable-macos-desktop, enable-windows-desktop, enable-android, enable-ios, cli-animations, enable-lldb-debugging [✗] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices [325ms] • Android SDK at /Users/herongkai/Library/Android/sdk • Emulator version unknown ✗ cmdline-tools component is missing. Try installing or updating Android Studio. Alternatively, download the tools from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only and make sure to set the ANDROID_HOME environment variable. See https://developer.android.com/studio/command-line for more details. [✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 15.3) [1,756ms] • Xcode at /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer • Build 15E204a • CocoaPods version 1.16.2 [✓] Chrome - develop for the web [22ms] • Chrome at /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome [!] Android Studio (not installed) [21ms] • Android Studio not found; download from https://developer.android.com/studio/index.html (or visit https://flutter.dev/to/macos-android-setup for detailed instructions). [✓] VS Code (version 1.103.1) [19ms] • VS Code at /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents • Flutter extension version 3.116.0 [✓] Connected device (2 available) [8.3s] • macOS (desktop) • macos • darwin-x64 • macOS 14.3.1 23D60 darwin-x64 • Chrome (web) • chrome • web-javascript • Google Chrome 139.0.7258.128 ! comments: @rk54rk thanks for the report. Could you please confirm whether #124966 is related to your case or not?

@tirth-patel-nc Hey thanks for the quick response!

As far as I see these seems to be different issues. #124966 says something is not working on MacOS. In my case the TextEditingController worked fine on MacOS, but on Web platform it is giving wrong composing TextRange.

Thanks for the report. Seeing the same behaviour with latest SDK versions on Web & iOS. ``` stable : 3.35.1 master : 3.36.0-1.0.pre-211 ``` --- ### Web & iOS ```console TextRange(start: 0, end: 1) TextRange(start: 0, end: 3) TextRange(start: 0, end: 2) ``` ### Android & macOS ```console flutter: TextRange(start: 0, end: 1) flutter: TextRange(start: 0, end: 3) flutter: TextRange(start: -1, end: -1) ``` --- | Platform | Video/Screenshot | | --- | --- | | **macOS** |
This issue might be related to: #128565 #113909

I think this is a regression likely caused by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/161593, I think its due to a misconception around the behavior between japanese and chinese text input. They behave slightly differently when inputting text. One key difference is that pressing the "space bar" seems to commit the text when typing chinese but not necessarily when typing japanese.
cc @Renzo-Olivares

On iOS with Chinese and Japanese keyboards, [setMarkedText](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinput/setmarkedtext(_:selectedrange:)) and [unmarkText](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinput/unmarktext()) create different composing events for the same string value. If you check the video attached to #177549 that fixed this behavior, you can see that `onChange` is being called twice. 

For example, they emit `TextRange(start: 0, end: 4)` and `TextRange(start: -1, end: -1)` for the string "テキスト". However, `onChange` in `EditableText` does not fire for the second event because the string value is still the same (still "テキスト").
Therefore, developers cannot detect when the composing range changes(e.g., when it becomes `TextRange(start: -1, end: -1)`).

This was previously discussed here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130081#issuecomment-1632968081
@koji-1009 Thanks! This works perfectly for my use case. Do you think I should close this issue? (not sure if the original platform behaviour inconsistency is still an problem)

@rk54rk 
Thank you for the mention.

I think this behavior is confusing, and I believe the root cause is the `TextField.onChanged` documentation's bias against composing languages.

https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/onChanged.html

The documentation's use of "typically" treats the standard IME input process as an edge case, and the current explanation forces developers to acquire platform-specific knowledge (like understanding `setMarkedText` and `unmarkText` events on iOS) just to correctly handle text input.

Therefore, I suggest updating the documentation to clarify the functional limitations of `onChanged` when dealing with composing text, and explicitly recommend using `TextEditingController.addListener()` as the proper solution for robust IME handling.

@Renzo-Olivares 
I feel it would be better to treat this as an issue for updating the documentation, rather than closing it at this time. What are your thoughts on this approach?

Thanks @koji-1009 for the excellent analysis and explanations! 

> I feel it would be better to treat this as an issue for updating the documentation, rather than closing it at this time. What are your thoughts on this approach?

I agree with your recommendation 👍

issues.title: [iOS] Switching focus to TextField with obscureText causes glitches on iPhone 13 Pro.
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Use an iPhone 13 Pro to reproduce the bug.
It may be linked to ProMotion.
I tried to reproduce it on iPad Air, but I didn't observe the bug.

It's kind of hard to see on video. But it's obvious with the naked eye.

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample.
2. Tap on the `Email` text field.
3. Tap on the `Password` test field.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The screen glitches.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
The screen doesn't glitch.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>


```dart
class MyHomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(title),
      ),
```

issues.title: D-pad navigation on TextField is broken - Android TV
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. create a new flutter project with `flutter create test_drive`
2. add a couple of TextFields
3. `flutter run` on Android TV device
4. select the text field
5. press the back button to close the keyboard
6. use the D-pad to change focus to another TextField/Widget

### Expected results

The D-pad navigation is working and the next widget/text field is focused

### Actual results

The use of the d-pad doesn't result in any action. Navigation is broken

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Column(
  mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
  children: <Widget>[
    const Text(
      'You have pushed the button this many times:',
    ),
    Text(
      '$_counter',
      style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headlineMedium,
    ),
    const TextField(),
    TextField(
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        filled: true,
        suffixIcon: IconButton(
          icon: const Icon(
            Icons.visibility,
          ),
          onPressed: () {},
        ),
      ),
    ),
  ],
),
```

</details>

@gspencergoog Looks like you added [navigationMode](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/MediaQueryData/navigationMode.html) years ago, do you know if it should handle TV remote input like this? I'm not aware of ever doing anything to support this kind of thing in our text fields, not sure if it has ever been supported or not.

That was the original intent, and it does affect the way text fields accept focus, etc.  For instance, `TextField` will accept focus even when it is disabled when in directional navigation mode.

It only handles TV remote input that emits directional arrows for the DPAD directions though (like Android TV does).  Perhaps something has broken this functionality, though.
I have exactly the same problem. Any update on this? Is there a workaround for this problem?
I have created a plugin hosting an native android textfiled as a workaround. [native_textfield_tv](https://github.com/zhuhaichao518/native_textfield_tv)

issues.title: Input disappears when using iphone to input Japanese into TextField in flutter web
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a project.

2.  Add the following code sample in main.dart.

3. Upload the app with firebaseHosting.

4. Access the uploaded page on your iphone. (Error confirmed with safari and chrome)

5. Type something in Japanese in the textField.

6. Break line in textField. Immediately after that, delete one character.

7. After that, type something and press the "confirm" button on the keyboard, and the text you typed will disappear.

### Expected results

After erasing the line break, the entered text will be entered as usual.

### Actual results

The entered text disappears.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(
  controller: TextEditingController(),
  maxLines: 5,
  minLines: 5,
  textInputAction: TextInputAction.newline,
  keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
),
```

</details>

comments: duplicate of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/28189
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/91804

I would like to reopen this since you the issue duplicate is a duplicate of another issue which is closed by one of your last PR. That PR does not fix this issue as I commented there.

> Note that ultimately this does not allow the text prefix icon to be position at the same text base line as the text:
> 
> This is with prefixIcon (with or without your fix):
> 
> ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22280985/137739399-11c4b79f-5968-4921-9c33-3140a7504b14.png)
> 
> This is with prefix which works
> 
> ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22280985/137740695-acb7b206-18b5-4e34-a60a-4faba190fec3.png)
> 
> This is important because there is no way to make the prefix to be always visible when unfocussed. This results in a behavior where when you don't have a label and you have an hintText, then the hinttext is placed in a weird position.
> 
> ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22280985/137740337-d9fb0380-6ea5-44eb-a9ca-b91588308d8f.png)
> 
> 
> I'll repeat myself at the risk of being obtuse but any fix that includes hardcoded values won't work for library makers as the people using said libraries can skew the alignment by: including a custon border, custom padding, whether there is a label, whether the border is outlined or underline, whether they run the library on x or Y platform.
> 
> In other words a solution needs to be found where you can have an icon shown at the same height as the text even when the input is unfocussed.

feel free to play around with https://github.com/cedvdb/phone_form_field there is an example you can run. `phone_field.dart` currently uses prefix, but I'd like an option to have `prefixIcon`. Because of the alignment it is blocked by this issue. It is not possible to align a prefixIcon for all platform, for all scenarios.

You can conveniently use the menu to see the alignments won't match

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22280985/137743033-69e4d2b7-66e4-4223-8214-6534d7053632.png)

I cannot furnish a valid solution to the users of my library that use an "hint" in the input. Please take a look at https://github.com/cedvdb/phone_form_field/issues/84 

Which is not acceptable for users. Since the flag is misaligned I chose to not show it at all but the "hint" is therefor looking extremely weird

@justinmc you asked me on a pull request what solution is envisageable. It's been some time that I looked into this but it would seem like the easiest fix would be to allow `prefix` to be shown at all time, since it is correctly aligned when focussed.

+1 as I have a similar issue without using @cedvdb 's package.

The `icon` property displays the same behavior as `prefixIcon`.
@AmitSonkhiya Take a look at the package, it is aligned now I don't remember how

> @AmitSonkhiya Take a look at the package, it is aligned now I don't remember how

@cedvdb The package has the `prefix` property to display the Flag/Code which gives the expected alignment but it is shown only when the input has the focus. Whereas I'm using the `prefixIcon` widget.

I have created a plain code with a `TextField` only that could be run to see the underlying issue with the widget.
[https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/108243](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/108243)
@AmitSonkhiya If there is no label and an hint it shown even when the phone number does not have focus (prefixIcon). It can easily be modified therefor to have an option to show it at all times.

@cedvdb 

Thanks for letting me know that.
I see that making `prefix` appear always is achieved through widget lifecycle events and `FocusNode`. 
I'm using StateLessWidget and currently, I have achieved the alignment using the `Transform` widget.
Through, I understand that it is not a reliable solution yet let me still give it a try.

After all, it would be great if the Flutter team could add an easy solution to attend the same using the `prefixIcon` property or an `alwaysDisplayPrefix` property.
As all the country flag/code/name packages are dealing with this issue using their own over-complicated hacky implementations.

Additionally, the solution [https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/91998](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/91998) doesn't resolve the text baseline positioning issue and feels more like a hack using `Align`, `Padding`, and forceful `widthFactor`, `heightFactor`. Nor I was able to fix the concern using the proposed solution.
This is immensely frustrating... I am just trying to align "Search" with the magnifying glass... i am losing my mind. I don't want to write my own widget for just this one thing.

Is there any progress on this issue?

I'm implementing a Design System where the labelHint needs to have the behavior of aligning itself above the icon, but I can't get this result in any way.

As a potential workaround, it seems as though `prefix` and `suffix` are displayed even when unfocused for an `InputDecorator`widget, so something like this does the trick in my case

```dart
InputDecorator(
  isFocused: widget.focusNode.hasFocus
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    prefix: _showPrefixWhenUnfocused ? widget.prefix : null,
    suffix: _showSufficWhenUnfocused ? widget.suffix : null,
  ),
  child: TextField(
    focusNode: widget.focusNode,
    decoration: InputDecoration.collapsed(
      hintText: widget.hintText,
    ).copyWith(
      focusedBorder: InputBorder.none,
      errorBorder: InputBorder.none,
      disabledBorder: InputBorder.none,
      enabledBorder: InputBorder.none,
      focusedErrorBorder: InputBorder.none,
      prefix: widget.showPrefixWhenUnfocused ? null : widget.prefix,
      suffix: widget.showSuffixWhenUnfocused ? null : widget.suffix,
    ),
  ),
)
```
Just wrap the prefix widget with a column and set the mainAxisAlignment property to MainAxisAlignment.center. That fixed the issue for me. Here, the "+91" text is the prefixIcon.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

I encountered this bug today and stumbled across something interesting while trying a few other ideas.  

Providing an empty BoxConstraints to the prefixIconConstraints of the TextFormField centered the icon nicely with no other text alignment needed.

Hope that helps someone.

Adding `canRequestFocus` is mainly because in the text field of `DropdownMenu`, if the menu options are limited, we want to provide an option so that we can only make selections and avoid the keyboard popping up.

@krida2000 Can I ask what you're trying to accomplish by setting canRequestFocus to false?  I dug into this and actually decided to remove this parameter in favor of using the FocusNode directly (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130164).  That still doesn't fix the buggy behavior, though.

@justinmc I wanted to remove the ability to select text. But if set `enabled: false` then `decoration.sufix` will no be clickable. Then I tried to disable selection by `canRequestFocus` because on desktop it works, but encountered with this bug.

Cursor overlap hint text when text aligned to end. The problem only occurs only on ios, on Android everything is fine.

## Steps to Reproduce

Code to reproduce:
```
void main() {
   runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
   @override
   Widget build(BuildContext context) {
     return const MaterialApp(
       home: Scaffold(
         body: Center(
           child: Padding(
             padding: EdgeInsets.all(60),
             child: TextField(
               textAlign: TextAlign.end,
               decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: "hint"),
             ),
           )
         ),
       ),
     );
   }
}
```

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Cursor should occur after hint text.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Cursor overlap last hint sign.

issues.title: iOS accessibility focus not returned to textfields after keyboard closed when other widgets are present
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable VoiceOver on iOS
2. Open flutter gallery app and navigate to material > text fields
3. Tap on any textfield, double tap to start editing
4. Dismiss the keyboard with done
5. The accessibility focus does not return to the edited textfield, but move to other widget and start to read (if there's something to read).

e.g 
Completes editing name textfield -> starts read Email textfield
Completes editing password textfield -> accessibility focus goes to submit button

This really causes a mess and makes it very difficult to complete multiple text inputs for VoiceOver users. But everything works fine on Android.

issues.title: TextField decoration documentation/assertion bug
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use Material `TextField` inside a custom theme, outside of `MaterialApp` and any other Flutter-provided wrappers
2. Set `TextField.decoration` to `null` as [per documentation](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html) **only having it non-null** requires you to have a `Material` ancestor:

   > If [decoration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/decoration.html) is non-null (which is the default), the text field requires one of its ancestors to be a [Material](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/Material-class.html) widget.

    Which is understood as having `decoration` value to be `null` allows you not to have `Material` ancestors

### Expected results

Everything works and no errors are happening

### Actual results

The error:

```
No Material widget found.

TextField widgets require a Material widget ancestor within the closest LookupBoundary.
```

So either documentation has to be changed or the assertion has to take into account the decoration value

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart' show DefaultCupertinoLocalizations;
import 'package:flutter/material.dart' show DefaultMaterialLocalizations, TextField;
import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(TextFieldExample());
}

class TextFieldExample extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(final BuildContext context) {
    return Localizations(
      locale: basicLocaleListResolution(null, [Locale('en', 'US')]),
      delegates: [
        DefaultMaterialLocalizations.delegate,
        DefaultCupertinoLocalizations.delegate,
        DefaultWidgetsLocalizations.delegate,
      ],
      child: TextField(decoration: null),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

_No response_

### Logs

_No response_

### Flutter Doctor output


comments:
@outring Thanks for filing this issue, please provide a minimal reproducible code so that we can assist you better.  
If possible, format `flutter doctor` output as per markdown syntaxes.

Hi @outring. Please can you provide a [minimal complete reproducible code sample](https://stackoverflow.com/help/minimal-reproducible-example) that doesn’t include 3rd party plugins or complex production code?
Thank you

Hi @dam-ease. I've updated the code sample. According to the documentation it should work without errors but in debug mode it causes an assertion failure here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/44a9b364617d20c2c7debaf22b89ce522eda208a/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1240

Either the assertion should take into account the value of `decoration` or the documentation should be updated to avoid confusion about the `Material` ancestors necessity. Thanks!
Hi @outring. Thanks for your response. Kindly check out the documentation https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/text-field-material-localizations on why you are getting this issue.

@dam-ease thanks. Please, check my code sample — it contains all the localizations (specifically it uses the method explained in the 3rd example of the page you have shared). The error I'm getting is:
```
No Material widget found.

TextField widgets require a Material widget ancestor within the closest LookupBoundary.
``` 

As you can see it says nothing about localizations. If I add a `Material` ancestor I won't get any errors at all. So the localizations have nothing to do with this

The problem is that per documentation you can use use `TextField` without `Material` ancestor if the `decoration` is `null`. Which is currently not true [per implementation](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/44a9b364617d20c2c7debaf22b89ce522eda208a/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1240)

Hi @outring. I think there might be a misinterpretation in the line you're referencing from the docs. From my understanding and from the error displayed, `TextField` widget requires a `Material` ancestor as also shown https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/text-field-material-localizations.

1. The documentation **doesn't say** that the `TextField` **requires** `Material` ancestor **at all times**. It only says that it's needed only when the decoration is not null:

    > If [decoration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/decoration.html) is non-null (which is the default), the text field requires one of its ancestors to be a [Material](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/Material-class.html) widget.

    So if you believe the implementation is correct, then the documentation has to be fixed to avoid future confusion

2. https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/text-field-material-localizations specifically doesn't say anything about `Material` ancestors, it only covers `Localizations` ancestor or a `MaterialApp` ancestor which has `Localizations` built-in. So I'm afraid it's not related to the issue I'm facing in any way

I would like to have a confirmation on the point 1, whether the documentation is wrong or implementation so I can prepare a PR for this issue, please

Hi @outring. Thanks for your patience and detailed explanation. From what's here, it seems the `textfield` applies the effectiveTextDecoration only when the `textDecoration` is not null. But then, if you don't want to use the Material, you could look into using EditableText directly so that you can customize it.

Here's where that assertion was added for the record: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/16147

I wonder if someone uses a TextField inside of CupertinoApp, does this assertion get triggered?

If someone (CC @Renzo-Olivares ) can put together a simple app that doesn't have a Material where TextField seems to work with the assertion disabled, then let's remove it.  Otherwise, if the TextField seems to break somehow, then we should remove the line in the docs.

This issue is assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!
This issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.

issues.title: Wrong vertical alignment of text on TextFormField widget if suffix widget is used.
issues.body: When I specify the `suffix` property for `TextFormField` the text in widget become not middle aligned by vertically. If searched on stackoverflow and found that [this issue](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/57659523/how-do-you-vertically-align-text-in-textformfield-with-a-suffix-in-flutter) is enough old. I don't mean _padding_ from left, I mean it is not aligned middle vertically. I tried to use `Padding` or `contentPadding` - it does not help.
comments: This seems to be a duplicate of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/42651 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/28189. If you agree please let's close this one and follow up on the mentioned issues as we don't track same issues on different places.

I don't think so. I read about `prefix` and `suffix` alignment and it is possible to fix it using `Padding` for those widgets. But this isssue is `text` related and there is no any possibility to fix it. Using `textAlignVertical` does not help.
P.S. Bug with age about 2 years?

```dart
import 'package:animations/animations.dart';

  runApp(MaterialApp(
    theme: ThemeData.light(),
    home: MainView(),
  ));
}

class MainView extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 200),
          child: TextFormField(
            textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words,
            autofocus: false,
            keyboardType: TextInputType.text,
            textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
            decoration: InputDecoration(
                contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
                filled: true,
                hintText: 'Enter your name',
                suffix: IconButton(
                  icon: Icon(Icons.keyboard),

}
```

issues.title: [Desktop app]Can not restrict candidate list on password field
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1、Built a password input box using TextField.
2、Switched the input method to Chinese.
3、Then entered the password in the constructed input box.

### Expected results

It cannot restrict the entry of Chinese characters and cannot automatically switch to English mode.

### Actual results

It cannot restrict the entry of Chinese characters and cannot automatically switch to English mode.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

/// Flutter code sample for [TextField].

class ObscuredTextFieldSample extends StatelessWidget {
  const ObscuredTextFieldSample({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return SizedBox(
      width: 250,
      child: TextField(
        inputFormatters: [
          FilteringTextInputFormatter.deny(RegExp("[\u4e00-\u9fa5]")),
        ],
        autofocus: false,
        obscureText: true,
        keyboardType: TextInputType.visiblePassword,
        textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
        decoration: InputDecoration(
          border: OutlineInputBorder(),
          labelText: 'Password',
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class TextFieldExampleApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const TextFieldExampleApp({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Demo')),
        body: const Center(
          child: ObscuredTextFieldSample(),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

void main() => runApp(const TextFieldExampleApp());

```

issues.title: Autofocus not granted in bottomsheet
comments:
@cedvdb 
Since you've set `autofocus: true`, the textfield will always have the cursor focus. If you apply a height to the bottomsheet container (ex: `height: 200`) and then tap on `show bottom sheet`, you'll notice that the cursor focus is in the textfield still and hence the bottomsheet's textfield didn't receive the focus.

`modalBottomSheet` is not same as bottomsheet and may not reflect same behavior. According to the [docs](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/BottomSheet-class.html),

`Modal. A modal bottom sheet is an alternative to a menu or a dialog and prevents the user from interacting with the rest of the app. Modal bottom sheets can be created and displayed with the showModalBottomSheet function.`

Hence, the behavior is different as compared to `bottomsheet`.

the autofocus is also set in my bottom sheet. Since it is in front it is expected to be focussed. 

The fact that it takes the whole page was to accentuate that fact, but even if it was of size 200 and had `autofocus true` it would be expected to take focus.

Imo, from an UX perspective, if it appears above the content and has autofocus, it should take focus. I can't imagine a scenario where that wouldn't be the case.

Thanks for the details. Keeping this open for further insights from team.

issues.title: ☂️ Proposal: desktop context menu fidelity
issues.body: The desktop text selection toolbars, as introduced in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/73882 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/74286, have major limitations that make them not as good as native. We should either find a way to use the native context menus, or we should work to fix each of these problems. * We can't draw the menu outside of the current window. Also, the menu can't receive mouse events (like hover) unless the current menu is focused. * The only buttons available are the default mobile buttons (cut/copy/paste/select all). We should support all of the same buttons that display natively. * It should be possible for Flutter devs to customize the menu. * The native Mac menu has a flashing animation when clicked. * Submenus should be possible and easy to implement. * Currently, only EditableText widgets can show the menu. Right-clicking elsewhere doesn't show it. Fixing this probably requires refactoring TextSelectionControls to work with both the existing setup and menus (like [showMenu](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/showMenu.html)). * Menu items that are wider than the default width are ellided, but on native the menu grows to accommodate them. * On Windows and Linux we use a Material-style menu, but we might want to build a menu that looks like the native one instead. * The native Mac context menu is slightly transparent and has a blur effect (screenshots below).
comments: Will this focus on Desktop Mac only? Adding labels just in case.

> * We should support all of the same buttons that display natively.

Doing this without using native menus would be non-trivial, due to [services](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/macos/extensions/services/)
Something missing from that list: Menus on macOS have a subtle glass effect.
@pedromassango This includes the Windows and Linux context menus as well.  I've updated the description to make that more clear and added the labels.
@shyndman What version of MacOS is that screenshot from?  I've been meaning to upgrade to Big Sur to check if it still looks like that but I haven't gotten around to it.

Ah, good point. I'm still on Catalina too. 

Only presuming it's the same, since their design system appears to be leaning even further into glass effects.

@justinmc, I confirmed on a Big Sur computer:

![0](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/42326/105408115-8b300b80-5bfc-11eb-8fa8-085825e8ea87.png)
@shyndman Thanks a lot for trying that out!  Added to the list above.

This has recently bitten us, as we have a styled right-click menu we built that matches our app, and we'd like to use it instead of this. 

Is there any mechanism to turn this off? It seems to be swallowing the secondary pointer event, not allowing it to bubble up.

@esDotDev Hmm we definitely want people to be able to turn this off.  Have you tried passing null to [selectionControls](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/selectionControls.html)?

Also, some general feedback on this topic, I think we're heading in the wrong direction trying to emulate the OS menus. 

If you look at most high profile cross platform apps on macOs, they almost all use app-specific context menus, (or at least menus that are not the "frosted glass"). 
* Slack
* Sublime Text
* Android Studio
* Spotify

All use app-specific context menus. From a developer perspective, they tend to:
* Match the app better
* Are much easier to test/debug
* Are much more flexible and easier to theme 
* Avoid the "uncanny valley" issues discussed here, where it feels slightly "off", this is generally worse UX than just having a bespoke menu.

Just seems like a nice, general purpose context menu **widget**, that can wrap anything, and be styled freely, is really the way to go. We built something recently like this, and it worked _very_ nicely. You can avoid any issues with over-hang on the window just using a quadrant-based positioning scheme.

From a framework SDK POV, these things will be ever-moving targets and very very hard to perfectly match. You'll end up with different OS dot releases, with subtly different expected behavior, and it will just be a bunch of technical debt. 

I guess the main thing is just that we need to be able to pass in our own background, and our own buttons, whereever context menus are used. Our own implementation of this is something like:

```
ContextMenuRegion(
   contextMenu: ImageContextMenu(url: url)
   child: SomeImage(url: url)
)
```
We can make ImageContextMenu, LinkContextMenu, TextContextMenu, AppContextMenu etc etc, super easy. We also have a GenericContextMenu which is very handy for quick implementions that are used only in one place in the app, for example:
```
return ContextMenuRegion(
      contextMenu: GenericContextMenu(
        labels: ["View", "Delete"],
        actions: [widget.isSelected ? null : widget.onPressed, _handleDeletePressed],
      ),
      child: innerContent,
    );
  }
```
All backed by basically 2 widgets, `StyledContextPanel` and `StyledContextButton`

I'm guessing that [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7b251f5f37b2b10334752da6bed1f5b136f0cb4b/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1313) is the GestureDetector that is swallowing your right clicks.  And [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7b251f5f37b2b10334752da6bed1f5b136f0cb4b/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L1233) is the definition of that GestureDetector.  If that's true, then I think we need something that gets rid of the secondary tap handlers on that GestureDetector, not just something to get rid of the selection controls.

I agree with you on allowing bespoke context menus.  I've been doing a bunch of work to allow the context menus to be customized on mobile, and I had planned to do the same thing on desktop, but we decided to keep everything private for now while our understanding of everyone's expectations for desktop matures a bit.

Currently, the TextSelectionControls setup that we've got is dependent on the EditableText-based widgets like TextField.  On desktop though, it's obvious that you need to be able to right click anywhere in the app and get a context menu to show up.  I think TextSelectionControls needs major refactoring, or we need to build something totally separate for desktop for now.

Thanks Justin, thats great to hear. We plan to make our `ContextRegion` a package, so I'll post that here when it's done as at least some reference (I described it a bit more above in an edit). 

Should I file another issue for the disabling of controls?
Yes please do and tag me in the new issue!  Thanks for the explanation in the comment edit.  I like that approach.

> Yes please do and tag me in the new issue! Thanks for the explanation in the comment edit. I like that approach.

Hey Justin, I've created a new issue for disabling here:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/79796

Somewhat relevant to this issue is an example of how we might want to style native menus:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/736973/113606909-d8b9f300-9605-11eb-8701-8e187961b1f9.mp4

Things to note here:
* we'd like total control over margins and paddings and heights of the btns
* we might want different hover colors depending on btn type (in our case, "delete" gets a special dark grey color, while the others are orange on hover)
* we need to disable an item based on some condition (in our case, "Set as Cover Photo" is disabled if this content is already the cover, or the content is not a Photo at all)
* We might want to assign an icon to a btn 
* We would like to use a non-system font to better match the application
* We'd like to provide our own divider padding, height and color

Popular apps where these sorts of custom (non native looking) menus can be seen are Spotify, Steam and Slack
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/736973/113607210-30585e80-9606-11eb-8ccc-237bf740717e.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/736973/113607242-38180300-9606-11eb-8b84-8cad2b217098.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/736973/113607309-4b2ad300-9606-11eb-8bb8-f3f4164fee5e.png)

Thanks for all of the examples!  I think all of this is doable in the long term.  I'd like to get a solution somewhat like what we have for Android with [TextSelectionToolbar](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextSelectionToolbar-class.html) and [TextSelectionToolbarTextButton](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextSelectionToolbarTextButton-class.html).

CC @shihaohong who I believe plans to work on nested menus.

Here are some strawman requirements I have gathered (I actually implemented one in some projects I am working on that fulfills exactly these requirements by copying the styling from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/8043d6723e04d9d68f98e2289f6fe6b36b22a0a9/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/desktop_text_selection.dart#L82 and the `LayerLink` approach from text selection):

1. Can be opened from any widget.
2. Text is completely customizable.
3. We have access to the offset (global/local).

This was very trivial to implement actually because 1. is already fulfilled by `Overlay` and the proper implementation using `LayerLink` and `CompositedTransformTarget` was already done by you @justinmc. Same goes for 3. because when we open the overlay ourselves, we have access to the global/local offset from whatever tap handler we are dealing with and we can transform it to the `Overlay`'s "global" coordinate system using:

```dart
final globalAnchor = (context.findRenderObject() as RenderBox).localToGlobal(
  localOffset,
  ancestor: Overlay.of(context, rootOverlay: true)!.context.findRenderObject(),
);
```

So the only thing I needed to do was 2. by customizing the `_DesktopTextSelectionControlsToolbar` widgets. (And of course there was some messy work trying to extract all of it from the specialized text selection use case to a general use case, where I also needed to make sure that the overlay is removed, the animation controller is handled properly, etc.)

fwiw there is no following behavior expected on desktop/web, resizing a window, or attempting to scroll, should close the context menu.

Edit - Actually, the behavior I described is only for web. Desktop apps sometimes follow, and sometimes close. The ones that follow seem to be electron based, native windows apps like task manager don't tend to let you resize and keep context menu open.
@esDotDev I've gotten around to refactoring the context menus to be based on plain widgets: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/107193

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/107193 has been merged and so the default desktop toolbars ([DesktopTextSelectionToolbar](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/desktop_text_selection_toolbar.dart) and [CupertinoDesktopTextSelectionToolbar](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/cupertino/desktop_text_selection_toolbar.dart)) are now public and customizable.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

issues.title: Undo/redo should coalesce changes correctly by platform
comments:
Hi @justinmc, Thanks for filing the issue. I could see there's a slight difference in how undo-redo works on native platforms and in the flutter app.   

### MacOS 

- On native platforms, each change seems to be related to delay between text entered if a text is typed fast without any delay whole text (fastly typed) seems to be removed on undo. While incase of flutter text typing doesn't seem to make a difference and undo operation looks more granular (always deletes a single/partial word never a set of words ) as compared to native platforms.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155958459-68d4f2f4-646f-4204-8a77-102c157f23b8.mov

- In the native macOS app sometimes a word is highlighted on undo (This is unclear when this happens) while this doesn't happen on the flutter side at all

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155958351-6c39f143-7bb5-4df1-8eff-66402de2cd51.mov


### Linux


On Linux undo seems to happen word by word regardless of how slow or fast you type, and Redo (ctrl+shift+z) doesn't seem to work in a flutter app

Flutter | Native
:-: | :-:
<video src='https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155959761-1e2210b8-57e6-4823-b1d1-77638c05ecac.mp4' width=180/> | <video src='https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155959784-68b52901-e56e-48ad-9424-9d482e3acaac.mp4' width=180/> 

- if you randomly type some text undo doesn't seem to remove the first set of characters added to stack.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155960751-ab2a62b3-7fd2-4ab4-ac52-319a7a288ada.mp4


### Windows

On the windows, platform Notepad seems quite complex in terms of how it handles the undo feature, I haven't been able to find the pattern here how windows handles undo feature. 

Flutter | Native
:-: | :-:
<video src='https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155968672-cc4e75ce-f40f-4f6a-bf90-641986a83158.mp4' width=180/> | <video src='https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155968774-072a760a-9335-406a-b563-72ab4f6963c3.mp4' width=180/> 


if we type random words (not meaningful) regardless of how fast or slow that is considered as a single change and so each undo operation deletes all the text regardless of how long it is.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155970388-4d8ad164-a967-42dc-afeb-0ecd0acfb5b6.mp4

Writing some meaningful sentence with a normal speed undo just removed the last character

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31410839/155970234-9458e574-b95d-405b-8644-e4f534e862c8.mp4

issues.title: No word-breaks for CJK locales
issues.body:
Internal: b/112618495

Flutter cannot properly detect word breaks in Chinese and Japanese text because the ICU configuration that we ship with Flutter doesn't include the dictionary to provide that functionality.

Chinese example:
Type 你好吗 into a text field. Long-pressing on either of the first two characters should select both characters as they form one word.

Japanese example:
Type 日本語学校 into a text field (means Japanese language school). Long-pressing on either of the last two characters should select them both to select the word school (学校).

Compare the result of the two experiments above between a native textfield and a Flutter text field to see the difference.

Other languages (e.g. English or German words) work fine and long-pressing a word selects the full word.

/cc @cbracken @xster @Hixie
comments:
Note: This data was missing from our ICU even before the recent attempts to reduce ICU size. The data is missing from Chromium's Android ICU configuration [1] that we have been using as the base for our ICU. The data is included in Chromium's common ICU configuration [2].

Adding the CJ dict required to detect Chinese/Japanese word breaks back to the ICU increases its size by 1.9MB (uncompressed) or 1.5MB (zip compressed).

[1] https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/deps/icu/+/master/android/
[2] https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/deps/icu/+/master/common/

> Hello from Korea.
> This issue is still reproducible on Flutter 2.2.3,
> I just realize reading a long paragraph without proper word-breaking is painful.

@dohuon  +1 here.

Consider [this comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59284#issuecomment-877904306)

@goderbauer you're probably most familiar with our ICU setup, but I imagine on desktop we aren't facing nearly the same size restrictions we face on storage/memory-strapped mobile devices.

Seems like there could be a couple options:
* Use a different version of ICU for desktop platforms
* Use a version of ICU where this data isn't stripped on all platforms

The second choice is probably a no-go, but I mention it since several years have passed since we stripped a ton of ICU in the name of space savings, and in the meantime, mobile devices will have added a bit more storage/memory, so possibly worth another evaluation. There are obviously still some very low-spec devices out there, especially in certain markets, so as I say, that option is likely a no-go.

Shipping different versions of the ICU for different platforms is what chromium did back in the days when I first looked at this (looking briefly at https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/deps/icu/+/refs/heads/main/README.chromium it still looks like they have different versions per platform). The desktop versions are larger and include more stuff. I imagine we could do the same.

The motivation behind making the ICU data on mobile as small as possible were concerns around app download size, not necessarily memory/storage issues.

> hi  @goderbauer 

We also had the same problem in Chinese.By Replacing the `//third_party/icu/flutter/icudtl.dat(800k)` with `src/third_party/icu/ios/icudtl.dat(10.2M)` can solve the problem but the app size it a little bit unacceptable for me. 
So I propose two ways to solve this problem:

1. Use the platform API to replace it when icult.dat lack of unsupported Chinese/Korean/Japanese locales.
    Liked iOS : [CFStringTokenizer](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/corefoundation/cfstringtokenizer?language=objc)
    Liked Andoird : [BreakIterator](https://developer.android.com/reference/java/text/BreakIterator.html)  It must use JNI(from C++ to call Java API)
2. Expose API the let developer choose the locales/feature when building the flutter engine.
    ICU builds the specified locale or features corresponding icuXX.dat file.

How about my proposals?

issues.title: Text selection toolbar is affected by TextField transformations
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `TextField` widget in a Flutter app.
2. Wrap it with a `Transform` widget (e.g. to scale or rotate the widget).

The use case here is a _drawing app_ that allows users to transform (scale & rotate) boxes with text and provides WYSIWYG input on the TextFields, regardless of where they are placed and how they are transformed. 

### Expected results

* The text selection toolbar should always be sized in screen coordinates, i.e. always have the same size, regardless of the transformation of the TextField.
* The text selection toolbar should always be drawn parallel to the screen's x-axis (i.e. perfectly horizontal), regardless of the transformation of the TextField.

Besides,

* Text selection should still work correctly. 

### Actual results

* The text selection toolbar scales and rotates with the TextField, i.e. it will potentially be drawn too big / small or rotated.
* Only parts of the TextField respond to tap events on a rotated TextField.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:math';

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: ConstrainedBox(
            constraints: const BoxConstraints(maxWidth: 300),
            child: Transform.rotate(
              angle: pi / 8.0,
              child: const TextField(),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

comments: Thanks for the report @PrimaryFeather If I understand correctly, the text selection toolbar text size should match with the text size being input in the textfield. Is this a correct interpretation of the issue ? I verified using latest master on Android as well as on iOS and saw below: 1. Android: 2. iOS: The size seems to match on Android, but on iOS the selection bar's text size seems a bit larger than the text entered. So, are you also seeing the same only on iOS ?

Thanks a lot for having a look at the issue, @darshankawar! I really appreciate it!

> If I understand correctly, the text selection toolbar text size should match with the text size being input in the textfield. Is this a correct interpretation of the issue ?

Almost: my understanding is that the selection toolbar buttons (and labels) should _always have the same size_ and be _horizontally aligned_ (not rotated), regardless of the TextField's transformation. 

E.g. look at how this is handled on iOS in Apple's "Freeform" app - see attached screenshots. The toolbar has the same size and orientation in both examples.

![IMG_6042](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c55f2305-fc21-4638-b67b-3425d2dd1a1a)
![IMG_6043](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e87de42c-b8d3-4839-a16c-e67c81e5eb8c)

I see what you are saying now. The toolbar need to be horizontal even if the text input field direction changes. Currently, the toolbar also moves / changes per direction of the textfield. 
This occurs both on Android and iOS as I verified [earlier](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/158263#issuecomment-2461580524).

```
stable : 3.24.4
master : 3.27.0-1.0.pre.424
```

> Only parts of the TextField respond to tap events on a rotated TextField.

Actually, I think this is just due to your ConstrainedBox! It's still axis aligned, but parts of the rotated field have overflowed it, and are not longer interactive. Remove that and see if your tap events work as expected.

@justinmc Thanks so much for looking into it, and for the detailed explanation! It's really appreciated! 🙏 

Your workaround is definitely of some help. Alas, it won't be as easy to apply in the real app; it's a drawing app and there's panning over the document area, i.e. the origin won't be the same for the context menu as what the transformation matrix points to. However, I'm sure there's a way to figure this out, so thanks a lot for pointing me at this! 🙌

> Actually, I think this is just due to your ConstrainedBox!

As for the hitTest regions - OMG, yes, you're right of course. If I move the _ConstrainedBox_ into the _Transform_ widget, hit tests behave perfectly fine. This also explains why I didn't run into _that_ issue in the original app. 🙉

In any case: yes, fixing this as the root (or at the many whack-a-mole holes you mention) would be the ideal solution; I'm sure I won't be the last one to run into this. However, I understand that the issue doesn't pop up in every typical app, of course.

So, to those other readers arriving on this page: please add your comment to help get some resources assigned for a solution. 😁

Again, thanks for all your input, @justinmc!

A real solution for this might just be a parameter in CompositedTransformFollower that says "only transform the translation, not everything else". `translationOnly`? Then inside of CompositedTransformFollower, you would strip the translation component out of the matrix returned by transformTo.

We'd have to make sure we're not adding a hack to CompositedTransformFollower just for this. I think this kind of functionality is probably useful even beyond context menu, though.
CC @victorsanni from triage in case you want to take a look.
Same issue, the popup menu should stay the same size, no matter if the textfield(or it's parent) is zoomed or not.

issues.body:
Having a long text in a TextField and trying to select a big portion of this is not possible by dragging.
Use the simple app in my sample code, then try to select a large portion of the text by drag.
Once the cursor reaches the bottom of the screen or the top of the keyboard, then the scroll jumps back to the start position and doesn't let you complete your selection.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Run the sample code below on DartPad.dev
2. try to select **from** the line ending with **"4" to** the line ending with **"200" By dragging**.

Expected:
Continue selecting the text smoothly by dragging.

Actual:
Showing the keyboard up (on Mobile) at the same time with dragging, and jumping scroll back to the start position of the selection.

<details>
<summary>Sample code</summary>

```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() =>
  runApp(MaterialApp(
    home: Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('a: TextField')),
      body: SingleChildScrollView(
        child: TextField(
          controller: TextEditingController(
            text: List.generate(300, (e)=> 'this is the text to be selected by dragging $e').join('\n')
          ),
          maxLines: null,
        ),
      ),
    ),
  ));
```
</details>

Hi @andromeda-center 
This issue is reproducible on the `stable` channel. But it seems to have been fixed on the latest `master` channel. 
Please try running app again on the latest master channel and confirm if this issue is fixed.

Hi @huycozy 
Yes, I see the scroll jumping has been fixed on the latest master channel.
**But the keyboard issue still bothers the user.**

When a user closes the soft keyboard manually and goes for a selection, it pops up immediately by starting drag.
While the user doesn't really need it during the selection.

A correct behavior could be like opening the keyboard after mouse-up, which means the user can complete their dragging and selection smoothly in a bigger viewport and then go for using the keyboard with comfort.

Thanks.

@andromeda-center Yes, I can see the keyboard issue still persists when start dragging the selection toolbar. It is reproducible on the latest `stable` and `master` channels. Labeling the issue for further investigation from the team.
